question: To which traffic does the SERA Regulation primarily apply? [Article 1(1), p.21]
Which of the following is specifically excluded from direct application of this Regulation? [Article 1(4), p.21]
Who is responsible for ensuring compliance with the common rules and provisions set out in the Annex? [Article 3, p.36]
Which activity is listed as one for which competent authorities may grant exemptions in the public interest? [Article 4(1), p.37]
What is the standard VHF emergency frequency referred to in Article 4a? [Article 4a(1), p.38]
What does 'altitude' mean in SERA definitions? [Article 2(39), p.24]
choices: Vertical distance measured from mean sea level
answer: Vertical distance measured from mean sea level

question: What does 'flight level' mean in SERA definitions? [Article 2(78), p.26]
choices: A surface of constant atmospheric pressure related to 1013.2 hPa
answer: A surface of constant atmospheric pressure related to 1013.2 hPa

question: What does 'height' mean in SERA definitions? [Article 2(84), p.26]
choices: Vertical distance measured from a specified datum
A level referenced only to mean sea level
answer: Vertical distance measured from a specified datum

question: What is 'minimum fuel' in the SERA definitions? [Article 2(94a), p.27]
choices: A distress condition requiring MAYDAY immediately
A state where the flight is committed to a specific aerodrome and no additional delay can be accepted
answer: A state where the flight is committed to a specific aerodrome and no additional delay can be accepted

question: What does 'special VFR flight' mean? [Article 2(122), p.30]
choices: A VFR flight in Class A airspace
A VFR flight cleared by ATC to operate within a control zone in meteorological conditions below VMC
answer: A VFR flight cleared by ATC to operate within a control zone in meteorological conditions below VMC

question: What does 'visibility' mean for aeronautical purposes under SERA? [Article 2(141), p.31]
choices: Only the distance a runway can be seen
Only flight visibility from the cockpit
The greater of object visibility against a bright background and light visibility against an unlit background
answer: The greater of object visibility against a bright background and light visibility against an unlit background

question: For flights over the high seas, which rules apply without exception? [SERA.1001(a), p.42]
choices: Only national rules of the State of registry

question: Without prejudice to SERA.1001, to which aircraft does the Annex apply? [SERA.2001, p.43]
choices: Aircraft operating into, within or out of the Union and EU-registered aircraft in any airspace where no conflict exists with local rules
Only EU-registered aircraft inside EU airspace
answer: Aircraft operating into, within or out of the Union and EU-registered aircraft in any airspace where no conflict exists with local rules

question: An aircraft on the movement area or in flight must comply with which set of rules? [SERA.2005, p.43]
choices: General rules and local provisions only
General rules, local provisions, and in flight either VFR or IFR
Only the rules published by ATC
answer: General rules, local provisions, and in flight either VFR or IFR

question: May a pilot elect to fly under IFR in visual meteorological conditions? [GM1 SERA.2005(b), p.43]
Who is responsible for the operation of the aircraft in accordance with SERA? [SERA.2010(a), p.43]
choices: The commander only when manipulating the controls
The pilot-in-command whether manipulating the controls or not
answer: The pilot-in-command whether manipulating the controls or not

question: When may the pilot-in-command depart from the rules of the air? [SERA.2010(a), p.43]
choices: When absolutely necessary in the interests of safety
answer: When absolutely necessary in the interests of safety

question: For which flights must pre-flight action include a careful study of current weather reports and forecasts? [SERA.2010(b), p.44]
choices: Flights away from the vicinity of an aerodrome and all IFR flights
answer: Flights away from the vicinity of an aerodrome and all IFR flights

question: Who has final authority as to the disposition of the aircraft while in command? [SERA.2015, p.44]
What does SERA say about psychoactive substances and safety-sensitive personnel? [SERA.2020, p.44]
choices: They may perform duties if under legal limits
They may not undertake the function while impaired and may not engage in problematic use of substances
answer: They may not undertake the function while impaired and may not engage in problematic use of substances

question: How may an aircraft be operated with regard to life or property of others? [SERA.3101, p.45]
choices: In any manner if clear of controlled airspace
Not in a negligent or reckless manner so as to endanger life or property
Only as approved by the operator
answer: Not in a negligent or reckless manner so as to endanger life or property

question: Except when necessary for take-off or landing, flights over congested areas must be at a height that permits what in an emergency? [SERA.3105, p.45]
choices: A turnback to the departure aerodrome
A landing without undue hazard to persons or property on the surface
answer: A landing without undue hazard to persons or property on the surface

question: Cruising levels are expressed in terms of flight levels when: [SERA.3110(a), p.46]
choices: At or above the lowest usable flight level or, where applicable, above transition altitude
answer: At or above the lowest usable flight level or, where applicable, above transition altitude

question: Cruising levels are expressed in terms of altitudes when: [SERA.3110(b), p.46]
choices: Below the lowest usable flight level or, where applicable, at or below transition altitude
answer: Below the lowest usable flight level or, where applicable, at or below transition altitude

question: Dropping or spraying from an aircraft in flight may only be conducted in accordance with what? [SERA.3115, p.46]
choices: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance
answer: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance

question: Towing by an aircraft may only be conducted in accordance with what? [SERA.3120, p.46]
choices: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance
answer: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance

question: Parachute descents other than emergency descents may only be made in accordance with what? [SERA.3125, p.46]
choices: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance
answer: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance

question: Aerobatic flights shall only be carried out in accordance with what? [SERA.3130, pp.46-47]
choices: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance
answer: Union or national legislation and relevant ATS information/advice/clearance

question: For formation flight, who must be designated? [SERA.3135(a), p.47]
choices: One pilot-in-command as the flight leader
answer: One pilot-in-command as the flight leader

question: For non-State aircraft in formation, what maximum vertical distance from the flight leader may be maintained? [SERA.3135(d), p.47]
What lateral and longitudinal distance limit applies to each non-State aircraft from the flight leader in formation? [SERA.3135(d), p.47]
Aircraft shall not be flown in a prohibited or restricted area except: [SERA.3145, p.47]
choices: In accordance with the conditions of the restrictions or by permission of the State concerned
answer: In accordance with the conditions of the restrictions or by permission of the State concerned

question: Nothing in SERA relieves whom from taking action to avert collision? [SERA.3201, p.47]
Regardless of the flight type or airspace class, what is important on board for detecting potential collisions? [GM1 SERA.3201, p.48]
How close may an aircraft be operated to another aircraft? [SERA.3205, p.48]
choices: As close as wake turbulence allows
Not so close as to create a collision hazard
answer: Not so close as to create a collision hazard

question: The aircraft that has the right-of-way shall maintain what? [SERA.3210(a), p.48]
An aircraft aware that the manoeuvrability of another aircraft is impaired shall: [SERA.3210(b), p.48]
choices: Maintain priority if on the right

question: When two aircraft are approaching head-on and there is danger of collision, each shall: [SERA.3210(c)(1), p.48]
When two aircraft are converging at approximately the same level, which aircraft shall give way? [SERA.3210(c)(2), p.48]
choices: The aircraft that has the other on its right
answer: The aircraft that has the other on its right

question: Power-driven heavier-than-air aircraft shall give way to which of the following? [SERA.3210(c)(2)(i), p.48]
An overtaking aircraft shall normally alter heading in which direction? [SERA.3210(c)(3), p.48]
Which aircraft may alter course either to the right or to the left while overtaking? [SERA.3210(c)(3)(i), p.48]
Which aircraft has priority when another aircraft is compelled to land? [SERA.3210(c)(4)(ii), p.49]
An aircraft taxiing on the manoeuvring area shall give way to which aircraft? [SERA.3210(c)(5), p.49]
choices: Aircraft taking off or about to take off
answer: Aircraft taking off or about to take off

question: At a controlled aerodrome, an aircraft taxiing on the manoeuvring area shall stop and hold at runway-holding positions unless: [SERA.3210(d)(2), p.49]
choices: An explicit clearance to enter or cross the runway has been issued
The pilot is uncertain of position
answer: An explicit clearance to enter or cross the runway has been issued

question: May a lit stop bar be crossed? [SERA.3210(d)(3) and GM1, pp.49-50]
choices: Yes, if the pilot has a crossing clearance

question: Which surface traffic has priority over all others when proceeding to the assistance of an aircraft in distress? [SERA.3210(d)(4)(iii), p.50]
If a pilot is in doubt as to the aircraft’s position with respect to the manoeuvring area, what should be done immediately? [SERA.3212(a), p.50]
choices: Stop the aircraft and notify ATS
answer: Stop the aircraft and notify ATS

question: If a pilot in doubt recognises the aircraft is on a runway, what should the pilot do if able to locate a nearby suitable taxiway? [SERA.3212(b), p.51]
choices: Remain on the runway until towed
Vacate the runway as expeditiously as possible unless otherwise instructed, then stop
answer: Vacate the runway as expeditiously as possible unless otherwise instructed, then stop

question: At night, all aircraft in flight shall display which lights? [SERA.3215(a), p.51]
choices: Anti-collision lights and, except for balloons, navigation lights
answer: Anti-collision lights and, except for balloons, navigation lights

question: All aircraft in flight fitted with anti-collision lights shall display them also during: [SERA.3215(c), p.51]
A pilot may switch off or reduce the intensity of flashing lights if they are likely to do what? [SERA.3215(e), p.52]
choices: Adversely affect duties or cause harmful dazzle
answer: Adversely affect duties or cause harmful dazzle

question: Simulated instrument flight may not be conducted unless which equipment is installed? [SERA.3220(a), p.52]
Under simulated instrument flight conditions, who must occupy a control seat? [SERA.3220(b), p.52]
choices: An additional qualified pilot acting as safety pilot
answer: An additional qualified pilot acting as safety pilot

question: Unless otherwise indicated or instructed by ATC, what turns shall aircraft make when approaching for landing and after take-off in the vicinity of an aerodrome? [SERA.3225(c), p.53]
Unless safety, runway configuration or traffic considerations determine otherwise, aircraft shall land and take off in which direction? [SERA.3225(d), p.53]
On the water, when two aircraft or an aircraft and a vessel are converging and there is risk of collision, which one gives way? [SERA.3230(a)(1), p.53]
choices: The one that has the other on its right
answer: The one that has the other on its right

question: Upon observing or receiving a signal given in Appendix 1, aircraft shall: [SERA.3301(a), p.54]
choices: Take the action required by the interpretation of the signal
answer: Take the action required by the interpretation of the signal

question: Which time standard shall be used in SERA? [SERA.3401(a), p.55]
Wherever time is utilised in data link communications, it shall be accurate to within: [SERA.3401(c), p.55]
A flight plan shall be submitted prior to any flight or portion thereof to be provided with what service? [SERA.4001(b)(1), p.56]
A flight plan shall be submitted prior to any IFR flight within what type of airspace? [SERA.4001(b)(2), p.56]
Unless otherwise prescribed, any flight across international borders requires what? [SERA.4001(b)(5), p.56]
Any flight planned to operate at night leaving the vicinity of an aerodrome requires what? [SERA.4001(b)(6), p.56]
How long before EOBT may a flight plan be submitted at the earliest? [SERA.4001(d)(1), p.56]
Flights that may be subject to ATFM measures shall be filed at least how long before EOBT? [SERA.4001(d)(2), p.56]
For flights not subject to ATFM measures, the flight plan shall be submitted at least how long before departure? [SERA.4001(d)(3), p.56]
If a flight plan is submitted during flight, it must be received at least how long before the aircraft reaches the intended point of entry into a control or advisory area? [SERA.4001(d)(4), p.57]
Which of the following is one of the required content items of a flight plan? [SERA.4005(a), pp.57-58]
Which of the following is one of the required content items of a flight plan? [SERA.4005(a), pp.57-58]
Which of the following is one of the required content items of a flight plan? [SERA.4005(a), pp.57-58]
Which of the following is one of the required content items of a flight plan? [SERA.4005(a), pp.57-58]
choices: Emergency and survival equipment, including ballistic parachute recovery system
answer: Emergency and survival equipment, including ballistic parachute recovery system

question: For a flight plan submitted during flight, what replaces the estimated off-block time? [SERA.4005(b), p.58]
choices: Time over the first point of the route to which the flight plan relates
answer: Time over the first point of the route to which the flight plan relates

question: An abbreviated flight plan transmitted by radiotelephony normally contains at least what? [GM1 SERA.4005(a), p.58]
choices: Call sign, type, point of entry, point of exit and level
Full flight plan including endurance and POB
Only the call sign and destination
answer: Call sign, type, point of entry, point of exit and level

question: Who must ensure that the content of the flight plan correctly reflects the operational intentions prior to operation of the flight? [SERA.4013(g), p.60]

All changes to a flight plan submitted for an IFR flight shall be reported during the pre-flight phase to whom for flights intended to operate IFR for part or all of the route? [SERA.4015(a)(1), p.60]
For other VFR flights, what changes to a flight plan shall be reported as soon as practicable? [SERA.4015(a), p.60]
If there is a delay of 30 minutes beyond EOBT for a controlled flight, what must be done? [SERA.4015(b), p.60]
choices: The flight plan shall be amended, or a new one submitted and the old one cancelled, as applicable
Only ATIS must be checked again
answer: The flight plan shall be amended, or a new one submitted and the old one cancelled, as applicable

question: For any IFR flight, delays of more than how many minutes shall be communicated to the Network Manager? [SERA.4015(b), p.60]
Incorrect fuel or energy endurance submitted prior to departure constitutes what? [SERA.4015(d), p.60]
choices: A significant change to the flight plan
A reason to cancel the flight automatically
answer: A significant change to the flight plan

question: When shall an arrival report be made after landing when required? [SERA.4020(a), p.61]
When is submission of an arrival report not required after landing? [SERA.4020(a)(1), p.61]
choices: When landing has been observed at an aerodrome where ATS are provided and indicated by radio communication or visual signals
answer: When landing has been observed at an aerodrome where ATS are provided and indicated by radio communication or visual signals

question: If no ATS unit exists at the arrival aerodrome or operating site, the arrival report shall be made how? [SERA.4020(c), p.61]
choices: As soon as practicable by the quickest means available to the nearest ATS unit
Only by telephone to the operator
answer: As soon as practicable by the quickest means available to the nearest ATS unit

question: Which of the following is an element of an arrival report? [SERA.4020(e), p.61]
Which of the following is an element of an arrival report? [SERA.4020(e), p.61]
At and above 10 000 ft AMSL, what minimum flight visibility applies in VMC? [SERA.5001 Table S5-1, p.62]
Below 10 000 ft AMSL and above 3 000 ft AMSL or 1 000 ft above terrain, whichever is higher, what VMC visibility applies? [SERA.5001 Table S5-1, p.62]
At or below 3 000 ft AMSL or 1 000 ft above terrain, whichever is higher, in Classes F and G, VMC may be: [SERA.5001 Table S5-1, p.62]
choices: 1500 m horizontally and 1000 ft vertically from cloud
Clear of cloud and with the surface in sight
answer: Clear of cloud and with the surface in sight

question: At and above 10 000 ft AMSL, what distance from cloud applies in VMC? [SERA.5001 Table S5-1, p.62]

When prescribed by the competent authority, reduced visibility to not less than 1 500 m may be permitted for certain low-speed or low-traffic operations at what maximum speed? [SERA.5001 footnote (***), p.62]
What lower visibility may helicopters be permitted to operate in, when so prescribed? [SERA.5001 footnote (***)(b), p.62]
Except as special VFR, VFR flights shall be conducted in conditions equal to or greater than what? [SERA.5005(a), p.63]
Without a clearance from ATC, VFR flights shall not take off, land, enter the ATZ or traffic circuit within a control zone when the reported ceiling is less than what? [SERA.5005(b)(1), p.63]
Without a clearance from ATC, VFR flights shall not take off, land, enter the ATZ or traffic circuit within a control zone when ground visibility is less than what? [SERA.5005(b)(2), p.63]
When VFR at night is permitted by the competent authority and leaving the vicinity of an aerodrome, what is required? [SERA.5005(c)(1), p.63]
When VFR at night is permitted and radio is available, the flight shall establish and maintain what? [SERA.5005(c)(2), p.63]
choices: Two-way radio communication on the appropriate ATS channel
answer: Two-way radio communication on the appropriate ATS channel

question: For VFR at night, what minimum ceiling applies? [SERA.5005(c)(3)(i), p.63]
For VFR at night below 3 000 ft AMSL or 1 000 ft above terrain, whichever is higher, in Classes B to G, the pilot shall maintain what? [SERA.5005(c)(3)(iii), p.63]
Except where specifically authorised, VFR flights shall not be operated above what level? [SERA.5005(d)(2), p.64]
When VFR flights above FL285 are considered, what condition prevents authorisation? [SERA.5005(e), p.64]
choices: When Class C exists above FL195
When 1000 ft vertical separation is applied above FL290
answer: When 1000 ft vertical separation is applied above FL290

question: Except when necessary for take-off/landing or with permission, what is the minimum VFR height over congested areas or open-air assemblies? [SERA.5005(f)(1), p.64]
choices: 500 ft above highest obstacle within 150 m radius
1000 ft above highest obstacle within 600 m radius
2000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km
answer: 1000 ft above highest obstacle within 600 m radius

question: Except when necessary for take-off/landing or with permission, what is the minimum VFR height elsewhere? [SERA.5005(f)(2), p.64]
choices: 500 ft above ground/water or highest obstacle within 500 ft radius
answer: 500 ft above ground/water or highest obstacle within 500 ft radius

question: VFR flights in level cruising flight above 3 000 ft from the ground or water, or a higher datum specified by the authority, shall be conducted at what? [SERA.5005(g), p.64]
choices: Any level chosen by the pilot
A cruising level appropriate to the track as in Appendix 3
answer: A cruising level appropriate to the track as in Appendix 3

question: VFR flights shall comply with Section 8 when operated in which airspace classes? [SERA.5005(h)(1), p.64]
A VFR flight in designated areas/routes shall maintain what on the appropriate channel? [SERA.5005(i), p.64]
choices: Listening watch only when crossing a boundary

question: What must a VFR aircraft wishing to change to IFR do when controlled airspace is involved? [SERA.5005(j), p.64]
choices: Submit or amend a flight plan as required and obtain a clearance prior to proceeding IFR in controlled airspace
answer: Submit or amend a flight plan as required and obtain a clearance prior to proceeding IFR in controlled airspace

question: Special VFR flights may be authorised to operate where? [SERA.5010, p.66]
choices: Within a control zone, subject to ATC clearance
answer: Within a control zone, subject to ATC clearance

question: Except where otherwise permitted for helicopters, special VFR flights may be conducted during: [SERA.5010(a), p.66]
Under special VFR, what minimum flight visibility applies for helicopters? [SERA.5010(b)(2), p.66]
Under special VFR, what speed limit applies? [SERA.5010(b)(3), p.66]
ATC shall not issue a special VFR clearance to take off or land within a control zone when the ceiling is less than what? [SERA.5010(c)(2), p.66]
Under IFR, aircraft shall be equipped with what? [SERA.5015(a), p.67]
choices: Suitable instruments and navigation equipment appropriate to the route
Only instruments required for VFR night
answer: Suitable instruments and navigation equipment appropriate to the route

question: Absent a published minimum flight altitude, what minimum level applies for IFR over high terrain or mountainous areas? [SERA.5015(b)(1), p.67]
choices: 1000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km
2000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km
2000 ft above highest obstacle within 5 NM
3000 ft above highest obstacle within 10 NM
answer: 2000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km

question: Absent a published minimum flight altitude, what minimum level applies for IFR elsewhere? [SERA.5015(b)(2), p.67]
choices: 500 ft above highest obstacle within 500 ft radius
1000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km
1500 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km
answer: 1000 ft above highest obstacle within 8 km

question: What exact phrase shall the pilot use to cancel IFR? [SERA.5015(c)(3), p.68]
Who may initiate a change from IFR flight to VFR flight? [SERA.5015(c)(3), p.68]
When in VMC, an IFR aircraft shall not cancel IFR unless what is anticipated and intended? [SERA.5015(c)(2), p.68]
choices: That the flight will continue for a reasonable period in uninterrupted VMC
That Class G airspace is ahead
That ATC agrees with no conditions
answer: That the flight will continue for a reasonable period in uninterrupted VMC

question: IFR flights in controlled airspace shall comply with which section? [SERA.5020(a), p.68]
An IFR flight cruising in controlled airspace shall normally fly at a cruising level selected from what? [SERA.5020(b), p.68]
choices: Table of cruising levels in Appendix 3
answer: Table of cruising levels in Appendix 3

question: An IFR flight in level cruising flight outside controlled airspace shall be flown at what cruising level? [SERA.5025(a), p.69]
choices: Any level chosen by the pilot
A cruising level appropriate to its track as specified in Appendix 3
answer: A cruising level appropriate to its track as specified in Appendix 3

question: When required in designated areas or routes outside controlled airspace, an IFR flight shall maintain what? [SERA.5025(b), p.69]
choices: Air-ground voice watch and establish two-way communication as necessary with the ATS unit providing FIS
answer: Air-ground voice watch and establish two-way communication as necessary with the ATS unit providing FIS

question: Outside controlled airspace, IFR position reports shall be made as specified for what, when required by the competent authority? [SERA.5025(c), p.69]
What type of flights are permitted in Class A airspace? [SERA.6001(a)(1), p.70]
In which airspace class are all flights separated from each other and all flights subject to ATC clearance while both IFR and VFR are permitted? [SERA.6001(a)(2), p.70]
In Class C airspace, VFR flights are separated from which traffic? [SERA.6001(a)(3), p.70]
In Class C airspace, what do VFR flights receive in respect of other VFR flights? [SERA.6001(a)(3), p.70]
choices: Traffic information and traffic avoidance advice on request
answer: Traffic information and traffic avoidance advice on request

question: In Class D airspace, IFR flights are separated from which flights? [SERA.6001(a)(4), p.70]
In Class D airspace, VFR flights receive what? [SERA.6001(a)(4), p.70]
choices: Traffic information in respect of all other flights and traffic avoidance advice on request
answer: Traffic information in respect of all other flights and traffic avoidance advice on request

question: In Class E airspace, which flights are subject to ATC clearance? [SERA.6001(a)(5), p.70]
Which class shall not be used for control zones? [SERA.6001(a)(5), p.70]
What service do participating IFR flights receive in Class F airspace? [SERA.6001(a)(6), p.70]
Is ATC clearance required in Class F airspace? [SERA.6001(a)(6), p.70]
In Class G airspace, IFR and VFR flights receive what if requested? [SERA.6001(a)(7), pp.70-71]
Above which level shall all airspace be classified as Class C? [SERA.6001(b), p.71]
What general speed limitation applies below 10 000 ft AMSL in Classes C to G, except where specifically approved? [SERA.6001(a)(3)-(7), pp.70-71]
Where vertically adjoining ATS airspaces of different classes meet, flights at a common level should comply with which class? [AMC1 SERA.6001, p.71]
Implementation of Class F should be considered as what? [SERA.6001(a)(8), p.71]
Before entering an RMZ, what must a pilot make on the appropriate frequency? [SERA.6005(a)(2), p.74]
choices: A blind transmission with squawk only
An initial call with station, call sign, type, position, level, intentions and other prescribed information
No call is required if monitoring
answer: An initial call with station, call sign, type, position, level, intentions and other prescribed information

question: All flights operating in a TMZ shall carry and operate what? [SERA.6005(b), p.74]
choices: SSR transponders capable of Modes A and C or Mode S
answer: SSR transponders capable of Modes A and C or Mode S

question: Which of the following is one of the objectives of air traffic services? [SERA.7001, p.82]
Which of the following is one of the objectives of air traffic services? [SERA.7001, p.82]
choices: Preventing collisions between aircraft on the manoeuvring area and obstructions on that area
answer: Preventing collisions between aircraft on the manoeuvring area and obstructions on that area

question: What should ATC do when a pilot reports a state of minimum fuel/energy? [SERA.11012(a), p.113]
choices: Issue MAYDAY on behalf of the aircraft
Inform the pilot as soon as practicable of any anticipated delays or that no delays are expected
Immediately clear the aircraft direct to the nearest aerodrome
answer: Inform the pilot as soon as practicable of any anticipated delays or that no delays are expected

question: When fuel/energy renders declaring a distress situation necessary, which radiotelephony distress signal shall the pilot use? [SERA.11012(b), p.113]
What does the phrase 'MINIMUM FUEL' tell ATC? [GM1 SERA.11012, p.113]
choices: All aerodrome options have been reduced to a specific aerodrome and additional delay may lead to an emergency
answer: All aerodrome options have been reduced to a specific aerodrome and additional delay may lead to an emergency

question: If aircraft performance is degraded below the level required for the airspace, what shall the flight crew do? [SERA.11013(a), p.113]
choices: Advise the ATC unit concerned without delay
answer: Advise the ATC unit concerned without delay

question: What special significance may a strayed aircraft and an unidentified aircraft have at the same time? [GM1 SERA.11010, p.113]
choices: They are always the same aircraft legally
An aircraft may be considered strayed by one unit and unidentified by another
Both terms apply only in military airspace
answer: An aircraft may be considered strayed by one unit and unidentified by another

question: Why is navigational assistance particularly important for a strayed aircraft? [GM1 SERA.11010(b), p.113]
choices: Because it may be nearing an area where there is a risk of interception or other hazard
answer: Because it may be nearing an area where there is a risk of interception or other hazard

question: What should ATS avoid doing in relation to an explosive device on board an unlawfully interfered aircraft? [GM1 SERA.11005(g), p.111]
choices: Providing any advice or suggestions on action to be taken by the flight crew concerning the device
answer: Providing any advice or suggestions on action to be taken by the flight crew concerning the device

question: What should a controller avoid saying unless the pilot has first said it on the radio during unlawful interference? [GM1 to AMC1 SERA.11005(a)(1), p.111]
An aircraft known or believed to be subject to unlawful interference should normally be cleared where? [GM1 SERA.11005(h), p.111]
choices: To the designated isolated parking position
answer: To the designated isolated parking position

question: Which of the following requires a special aircraft observation? [SERA.12005(a), p.128]
Which of the following requires a special aircraft observation? [SERA.12005(a), p.128]
Which thunderstorm situations require special aircraft observations? [SERA.12005(a)(4)-(5), p.128]
choices: Obscured, embedded, widespread or squall-line thunderstorms, with or without hail as specified
answer: Obscured, embedded, widespread or squall-line thunderstorms, with or without hail as specified

question: Which runway-related condition requires a special aircraft observation? [SERA.12005(a)(9), p.128]
choices: Runway braking action not as good as reported
answer: Runway braking action not as good as reported

question: When other meteorological conditions not listed, such as wind shear, are encountered and may affect safety, what shall the pilot-in-command do? [SERA.12010, p.128]
choices: Advise the appropriate ATS unit as soon as practicable
answer: Advise the appropriate ATS unit as soon as practicable

question: When shall aircraft observations be reported? [SERA.12015(a), p.128]
choices: At the end of the flight
At the time observed or as soon thereafter as practicable
answer: At the time observed or as soon thereafter as practicable

question: Air traffic services units shall transmit special and non-routine air-reports as soon as practicable to whom? [SERA.12020(a), p.128]
choices: Other aircraft concerned, the associated MWO, and other ATS units concerned
answer: Other aircraft concerned, the associated MWO, and other ATS units concerned

question: Aircraft equipped with a Mode S or ADS-B transmitter having an aircraft identification feature shall transmit what identification? [SERA.13015(a), pp.132-133]
choices: The aircraft identification as specified in the flight plan, or if none filed, the registration unless approved otherwise
answer: The aircraft identification as specified in the flight plan, or if none filed, the registration unless approved otherwise

question: If transmitted aircraft identification differs from that expected, what shall the pilot be requested to do? [SERA.13015(b), pp.132-133]
choices: Confirm and, if necessary, re-enter the correct aircraft identification
answer: Confirm and, if necessary, re-enter the correct aircraft identification

question: If the aircraft identification discrepancy persists after pilot confirmation, what shall ATS do where possible? [SERA.13015(c)(2), p.133]
choices: Correct the label showing the aircraft identification on the situation display
Switch the aircraft to another frequency
answer: Correct the label showing the aircraft identification on the situation display

question: An aircraft equipped with ADS-B may use emergency or urgency mode to indicate which of the following? [GM1 SERA.13015, p.134]
An aircraft equipped with ADS-B may use emergency or urgency mode to indicate which of the following? [GM1 SERA.13015, p.134]
If displayed level information from pressure-altitude-derived data is not within approved tolerance, what should the pilot first be asked to check? [GM1 SERA.13010(b), p.132]
choices: Pressure setting and confirm the aircraft's level
answer: Pressure setting and confirm the aircraft's level

question: If after correct pressure setting confirmation the displayed level discrepancy continues, ATC may request the pilot to do what, if available? [GM1 SERA.13010(b)(b)(1), p.132]
choices: Select an alternative transponder and re-verify
answer: Select an alternative transponder and re-verify

question: Why must controllers consider ACAS if erroneous Mode C information cannot be corrected? [GM1 SERA.13010(b)(c), p.132]
choices: Because ACAS may generate an RA based on erroneous inputs
Because ACAS cannot be used in controlled airspace
answer: Because ACAS may generate an RA based on erroneous inputs

question: Standardised phraseology shall be used in all situations for which it has been specified. What is used only when that phraseology cannot serve an intended transmission? [SERA.14001, p.136]
During flight, aircraft shall maintain watch as required by the competent authority and shall not cease watch except for what reason, without informing ATS? [SERA.14080(a), p.198]
Aircraft on long over-water flights or flights over designated areas where carriage of an ELT is required shall continuously guard which frequency? [SERA.14080(a)(1), p.198]
When an aircraft is unable to comply with SERA.8035(a), what shall the flight crew first attempt? [SERA.14083(a), p.198]
choices: To establish contact on the previous channel used and, if not successful, on another appropriate channel
answer: To establish contact on the previous channel used and, if not successful, on another appropriate channel

question: If attempts on the assigned or appropriate channels fail after radio communication failure, the flight crew shall attempt to establish communication with whom using all available means? [SERA.14083(a), p.198]
choices: The appropriate ATS unit, other ATS units, or other aircraft
answer: The appropriate ATS unit, other ATS units, or other aircraft

question: On radio communication failure, what transponder code shall be set? [SERA.14083(c)(2), p.199]
After radio communication failure, a VFR flight shall do what? [SERA.14083(c)(3), p.199]
choices: Continue to destination regardless of weather
Continue in VMC, land at the nearest suitable aerodrome, and report arrival by the most expeditious means
Climb to the MSA and hold
answer: Continue in VMC, land at the nearest suitable aerodrome, and report arrival by the most expeditious means

question: Following radio communication failure, how long shall an IFR flight maintain the last assigned speed and level, or minimum flight altitude if higher, after a required report is missed or code 7600 is set while surveillance service is provided? [SERA.14083(c)(4)(i), p.199]

If being vectored or directed offset by RNAV when radio communication fails, and no specified limit was given, by when shall the aircraft rejoin the last received and acknowledged route? [SERA.14083(c)(4)(ii)(B), p.199]
choices: No later than the next significant point
answer: No later than the next significant point

question: Following radio communication failure, an IFR flight shall commence descent from the designated navigation aid or fix at, or as close as possible to, which time if no expected approach time has been received? [SERA.14083(c)(4)(iv), p.199]
After radio communication failure, if an IFR flight encounters VMC and the pilot decides to continue in VMC, what Mode A code shall be set? [SERA.14083(c)(6), p.199]
What distinguishes a distress condition from an urgency condition? [SERA.14095(a)(1), p.207]
choices: Distress requires immediate assistance; urgency does not
Urgency requires immediate assistance; distress does not
Distress is only for medical flights
answer: Distress requires immediate assistance; urgency does not

question: Which signal shall be used at the commencement of the first distress communication? [SERA.14095(a)(2), p.207]
Which signal shall be used at the commencement of the first urgency communication? [SERA.14095(a)(2), p.207]
Urgency communications have priority over all other communications except what? [SERA.14095(c)(3), p.210]
What additional signal follows 'PAN PAN' for protected medical transport communications? [SERA.14095(c)(4)(ii), p.210]
Which of the following is one of the permitted genuine emergency uses of 121.500 MHz? [SERA.14095(d), pp.211-212]
choices: A common VHF channel in the event of interception of a civil aircraft
answer: A common VHF channel in the event of interception of a civil aircraft

question: Which of the following is one of the permitted genuine emergency uses of 121.500 MHz? [SERA.14095(d), pp.211-212]
choices: A clear channel between aircraft in distress or emergency and a ground station when normal channels are busy
answer: A clear channel between aircraft in distress or emergency and a ground station when normal channels are busy

question: What should be avoided when using 121.500 MHz for common search and rescue operations? [GM1 SERA.14095(d)(3), p.213]
choices: Interfering in any way with the efficient handling of distress traffic
answer: Interfering in any way with the efficient handling of distress traffic

question: Upon identifying a suspected communicable disease or other public health risk on board, whom shall the flight crew promptly notify? [SERA.14100(a), p.213]
choices: The ATS unit with which the pilot is communicating
answer: The ATS unit with which the pilot is communicating

question: Which of the following must be included in the notification of a suspected communicable disease? [SERA.14100(a), p.213]
Which of the following must be included in the notification of a suspected communicable disease? [SERA.14100(a), p.213]
choices: Number of suspected cases on board
answer: Number of suspected cases on board

question: Which symptom combination may indicate a suspected communicable disease requiring further evaluation? [GM1 SERA.14100, p.213]
choices: Fever of 38 °C/100 °F or greater with other concerning symptoms
answer: Fever of 38 °C/100 °F or greater with other concerning symptoms
